componentDescription: AvatarGroup displays multiple Avatar components in a stacked/overlapping row with an overflow count. Use it to show the members of a team, the assignees of a task, or the participants of a meeting when horizontal space is limited.
features: Overlapping stack layout for space efficiency
Overflow count badge when avatars exceed the max display limit
Consistent sizing applied to all avatars in the group
Tooltip on each avatar showing the user's name
compositionPattern: Pass an array of avatar data objects (each with src and name) to the avatars prop. The component handles overlap layout and overflow counting automatically.

usageExamples.title: Task assignees
usageExamples.description: Show up to 3 assignees with overflow count for a task card.
usageExamples.code:
<AvatarGroup
  avatars={task.assignees.map(a => ({ src: a.avatarUrl, name: a.name }))}
  max={3}
  size="sm"
/>

usageExamples.title: Team members in a project header
usageExamples.description: Show all team members, stacked with overlap.
usageExamples.code:
<AvatarGroup
  avatars={project.members.map(m => ({ src: m.photo, name: m.fullName }))}
  max={5}
  size="md"
/>

avatars.llmContext: Required. Array of avatar data objects. Each should have at minimum name (for initials/alt text) and optionally src (image URL). Passed in the order they should appear.
avatars.propDescription: Required. Array of avatar data objects with src and name

max.llmContext: Maximum number of avatars to display before showing an overflow count badge. Set based on available space — typically 3-5. The overflow badge shows '+N' for the remaining count.
max.propDescription: Maximum number of avatars shown before overflow count

size.llmContext: Applied uniformly to all avatars in the group. Use 'sm' for compact cards and table cells. Use 'md' for standard card headers and list items.
size.propDescription: Size applied to all avatars in the group
